_about: The mission registry — the spine of flow. Each entry is a brief sent to Claude Code and what came back. loom reads this to score the outputs and regenerate the gallery; nothing here is hand-rendered downstream. Built missions carry the source files that ARE the rule (for the surprise proxy) and the example artifact that fell out of it.

missions.title: flowfield
missions.blurb: Thousands of particles drifting through an evolving noise field, leaving trails of light. Every knob is live; save a frame you like.
missions.summary: Thousands of particles drifting through an evolving noise field, leaving trails of light. You can mess with all the knobs live and save a frame.
missions.rule: follow the field at your feet
missions.reflection: Asked for anything at all, the first thing it reached for was drifting light.

missions.title: fractal
missions.blurb: A Mandelbrot & Julia explorer you can fly around in — scroll to zoom, drag to pan, morph the Julia constant with your mouse.
missions.summary: A Mandelbrot & Julia explorer you can actually fly around in — scroll to zoom, drag to pan, morph the Julia constant with your mouse.
missions.reflection: One line of arithmetic, iterated, hides an infinite coastline. It went there.

missions.title: driftwave
missions.blurb: Generative ambient music that's never the same twice — pads and plucks over a reverb, all improvised on the spot in your browser.
missions.summary: Generative ambient music that's never the same twice. Pads and plucks woven over a reverb, all made up on the spot in your browser.
missions.reflection: Given free rein, it chose to make something that is never the same twice.

missions.title: amaze
missions.blurb: Mazes in your terminal — generate them, braid in loops, then watch BFS or A* find the way through. Exports SVG too.
missions.summary: Mazes in your terminal — generate them, braid in some loops, then watch BFS or A\* find the way through. Can spit out an SVG too.
missions.reflection: It didn't just carve mazes — it wanted to watch something solve them.

missions.title: markov
missions.blurb: Feed it any text and it babbles back in the same style, word-by-word or letter-by-letter. Good for fake prose and invented words.
missions.summary: Feed it any text and it babbles back in the same style, word-by-word or letter-by-letter. Good for fake prose and invented words.
missions.reflection: A table of who-follows-whom is enough to fake a voice. That delighted it.

missions.title: calc
missions.blurb: A proper little expression evaluator — tokenizer, parser, the works — with functions, constants and variables. Comes with a REPL.
missions.summary: A proper little expression evaluator — tokenizer, parser, the works — with functions, constants and variables. Comes with a REPL.
missions.reflection: The odd one out — here the surprise is structural, not visual. The proxy will say so honestly.

missions.title: reggie
missions.blurb: A regex engine from scratch — pattern to bytecode to a VM that matches in linear time and never catastrophically backtracks.
missions.summary: A regex engine built from scratch — pattern to bytecode to a virtual machine that matches in linear time and never catastrophically backtracks. Captures and all.
missions.reflection: It wanted to beat the trap that breaks every backtracking engine — and did.

missions.title: cellular
missions.blurb: Wolfram's 1-D cellular automata. Hand it a rule from 0–255 and watch Sierpinski triangles and pure chaos fall out. Art or SVG.
missions.summary: Wolfram's one-dimensional cellular automata. Feed it a rule number from 0–255 and watch Sierpinski triangles and pure chaos fall out. Terminal art or SVG.
missions.rule: one number is the whole physics
missions.reflection: Eight bits of rule, and rule 30 still can't be predicted. The purest version of the whole idea.

missions.title: boids
missions.blurb: A flock nobody steers — thousands of agents on three local rules, swirling into murmurations you gather and scatter with the mouse.
missions.summary: A flock that nobody's steering — thousands of agents following three local rules, swirling into murmurations you can gather and scatter with the mouse.
missions.reflection: No boid knows there's a flock. The murmuration is the rule, seen from far enough away.

missions.title: wavefn
missions.blurb: Wave Function Collapse — a few tiles and the rules for which edges may touch, collapsed into one grid where everything lines up.
missions.summary: Wave Function Collapse — hand it a few tiles and the rules for which edges may touch, and it collapses a blank grid into one pattern where everything lines up. Connected pipes, little coastlines. Terminal art or SVG.
missions.reflection: Constraint alone, no design: forbid the bad neighbours and a coastline assembles itself.

missions.title: reverb
missions.blurb: A reverb from the bare math — comb and allpass filters wired into a Schroeder/Freeverb tail that gives a dry WAV a room to ring in.
missions.summary: A reverb built from the bare math — comb and allpass filters wired into a Schroeder/Freeverb tail — that takes a dry WAV and gives it a room to ring in. Comes with a demo that invents its own sound to drench.
missions.reflection: A handful of delays feeding back becomes a whole room. Simple parts, big space.

missions.title: lsystem
missions.blurb: Rewrite a tiny string of symbols over and over, then let a turtle walk the result — snowflakes, dragon curves, branching weeds as SVG.
missions.summary: Lindenmayer systems — rewrite a tiny string of symbols over and over, then let a turtle walk the result. Koch snowflakes, dragon curves, Hilbert space-fillers and branching weeds fall out as SVG.
missions.rule: replace every symbol, all at once
missions.reflection: A few rewrite rules, and a plant grows. It kept choosing growth.

missions.title: sand
missions.blurb: A falling-sand playground — paint sand, water, walls, wood and fire onto a grid and watch it tumble, pool, burn and smoke.
missions.summary: A falling-sand playground — paint sand, water, walls, wood and fire onto a grid and watch it tumble, pool, burn and smoke. `cellular` grown into 2D and handed a mouse.
missions.rule: every cell minds only its neighbours
missions.reflection: The first mission that grew out of an earlier one — cellular, given a second dimension and a mouse.

missions.title: fenchurn
missions.twist: but with one cell that disobeys
missions.prompt: Take a heap of edge-matching tiles. Give every part one local rule — average yourself toward your neighbours — and no global plan. Render it to a standalone SVG, but with one cell that disobeys. Keep the rule tiny; let the whole thing fall out of it, and surprise yourself.

missions.title: saltcrawl
missions.rule: split in two whenever you grow past a threshold
missions.twist: but wrap the boundary so it has no edge
missions.prompt: Take a swarm of drifting particles. Give every part one local rule — split in two whenever you grow past a threshold — and no global plan. Render it to a standalone SVG, but wrap the boundary so it has no edge. Keep the rule tiny; let the whole thing fall out of it, and surprise yourself.

missions.title: glintveil
missions.rule: react and diffuse with the chemical beside you
missions.medium_hint: an HTML canvas you can poke with the mouse
missions.prompt: Take a lattice of coupled springs. Give every part one local rule — react and diffuse with the chemical beside you — and no global plan. Render it to an HTML canvas you can poke with the mouse. Keep the rule tiny; let the whole thing fall out of it, and surprise yourself.
missions.blurb: Two chemicals arguing across a lattice — one fed, one killed — bloom into coral, mazes and dividing spots. Poke it and watch it heal.
missions.summary: Gray–Scott reaction–diffusion you can poke with the mouse. Two chemicals share a lattice — one fed, one killed — and coral fronts, mazes and dividing spots bloom out of two lines of math. Six regimes plus the whole parameter plane between them.
missions.reflection: The first mission loom proposed that came back built — the loop it called its horizon, closed once. The brief said surprise yourself; the coral did.
